explanation: Production overview that passed the v1 rubric. Under the v2 rules it fails on smart-brevity / format: the Linear Agent + MCP section packs four distinct concrete items into a single 60-word comma-separated paragraph (MCP servers list, allowlists, triage automations, Code Intelligence) — that should be bullets per the new sentence-list-to-bullets rule. The Microsoft Teams section is similarly dense. Voice and weighting are fine; bold leads describe user-facing changes; word count is in range (241). The fix is to convert dense multi-item sections to tease + bullets.

criteria.name: Sections with 3+ concrete items should bullet, not prose
criteria.evidence: The Linear Agent + MCP section packs four distinct items (MCP server connections, allowlist gating, triage automations, Code Intelligence rollout) into one paragraph; the Microsoft Teams section similarly bundles bot mentions, custom launchers, and a five-tool deeplink list.

criteria.name: A sentence with four or more comma-separated items should be bullets
criteria.evidence: `Granola, Glean, Notion, PostHog` and `Amp, Devin, Factory, Warp, Windsurf, and others` are both four-plus-item comma lists that read better as bullets.
